name: Rolling-summary demo
description: A native 'every-10-turns' summary agent that writes a rolling summary to a Blackboard VARIABLE, plus two reader agents that consume that variable in their prompts. Demonstrates: mod-on-turn_count gating, variable_updates, and cross-agent variable reads. The summarizer's prompt is a portable template for the real xubb app.

agents.name: Conversation Summarizer
agents._doc: THE STAR. Fires ONLY when turn_count % 10 == 0 (the mod condition) — i.e. turns 10 and 20 here. Writes the rolling `summary` VARIABLE (no whisper). On every other turn it appears under 'skipped: conditions_not_met'. This is the native 'every-N-turns' pattern: a turn_based agent gated by {meta:turn_count, op:mod, value:10}.

variable_updates.summary: Summary (through turn 10): YOU opened and ran discovery. SPEAKER described a manual, spreadsheet-based workflow with ~10 hrs/week lost; ~5 people on the ops team affected. Phase: discovery. No pricing or objections yet. Open: surface budget + timeline, then transition to solution.

Summary (through turn 20): Discovery done (spreadsheet pain, ~10 hrs/week, 5-person ops team). Pricing introduced; SPEAKER raised a cost objection and YOU anchored on ROI. Budget ~= $40k/yr signaled; a buying signal emerged (~turn 18). Phase: negotiation -> closing. Open: confirm fit, propose pilot + 2-week timeline, send proposal.

agents.name: Context-Aware Coach
agents.text:
[Speaker roles] "YOU" is the user you privately coach; every other speaker is the audience / counterparty.

You coach YOU using the WHOLE conversation, not just the last line. Use the running summary below so your advice fits the broader context.

Running summary: {{ blackboard.variables.summary }}

Only whisper when you have a high-value, context-aware suggestion for YOU based on the latest turn AND the summary. Otherwise has_insight=false. Address YOU directly ("you...", "ask them...").
agents._doc: READER #1. Its prompt embeds {{ blackboard.variables.summary }} — open 'view exact prompt' on this agent after turn 10 and you'll see the rolling summary rendered right into the prompt it actually received. Proof of cross-agent variable access.

speak.content: Reframe on ROI using what they already told you — ~10 hrs/week lost across 5 people. Tie price to that, not to their current tool.

agents.name: Deal Advisor
agents.text:
[Speaker roles] "YOU" is the user you coach; every other speaker is the audience / counterparty.

You advise YOU on advancing the deal, informed by the full conversation.

Running summary: {{ blackboard.variables.summary }}

When the audience signals readiness, prompt YOU to propose concrete next steps grounded in the summary's facts. Otherwise stay silent (has_insight=false).
agents._doc: READER #2 — also consumes the `summary` variable in its prompt. Two agents reading the same shared summary = the variable is accessible to other agents, as designed.

speak.content: Strong signal. Propose a pilot with a 2-week timeline and reference their ~$40k/yr budget from earlier.
